activities_projects.title: Helen Keller’s Perspective
activities_projects.objective: Understand the author's background and unique viewpoint
steps: Read the introduction to the chapter 'Three Days to See'
Identify who Helen Keller was and what senses she lacked
Write three sentences about why she wants to 'see' for three days
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy and understand the author's context
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the author's challenges and motives (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The First Day: People and Faces
activities_projects.objective: Identify the people Helen Keller values most
steps: Read the section describing the first day
List the people Helen wants to see on this day
Write down why she wants to look into her teacher's (Anne Sullivan's) eyes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the importance of human connection and gratitude
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in identifying key people and their significance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: A 'Walk' Through Nature
activities_projects.objective: Observe details in nature that we often take for granted
steps: Close your eyes and touch a leaf or flower carefully
Describe the texture (smooth, rough, ribbed) in two sentences
Find a line in the text where Helen describes the 'quivering' of a tree
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice sensory observation beyond just sight
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of sensory description and textual reference (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Gift of Sight: A List
activities_projects.objective: Appreciate the everyday things we see
steps: Look around your classroom or home
List 5 simple things you see that you think Helen Keller would find beautiful
Share your list with a classmate and explain why you chose one item
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students cultivate gratitude for their senses
activities_projects.evaluation: Thoughtfulness and relevance of the list (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Light and Shadow
activities_projects.objective: Learn words related to sight and perception
steps: Find the words 'Glimpse', 'Panorama', 'Tactile', and 'Dusk' in the chapter
Write the meaning of each word in simple English
Fill in the blanks: 'I caught a ___ of the bird before it flew away.'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students build vocabulary related to the theme of vision
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and sentence completion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Describing the Second Day
activities_projects.objective: Understand the cultural and historical interests of the author
steps: Identify where Helen wants to go on the second day (e.g., museums, theaters)
Write 3 sentences describing what she hopes to learn from seeing art and history
Draw a small picture of a museum object she might want to see
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the value of art and history in human life
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of description and relevance of the drawing (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Third Day: City Life
activities_projects.objective: Observe the 'business of life' in a busy place
steps: Read about Helen's third day in the city
List three sounds or sights she mentions about city life
Write one sentence on how she feels about seeing 'the work-a-day world'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to find beauty in everyday activities
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in listing details from the text (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identifying 'Sense' Verbs
activities_projects.objective: Recognize verbs related to the five senses
steps: Go through the first two pages of the chapter
Find 3 verbs related to 'See', 2 for 'Touch', and 1 for 'Hear'
Write them down in a neat table
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify parts of speech in context
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and categorization of verbs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: A Message to Others
activities_projects.objective: Identify the moral lesson of the story
steps: Find the part of the text where Helen gives advice to people who can see
Copy her main advice in your best handwriting
Explain in your own words what 'Use your eyes as if tomorrow you would be stricken blind' means
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students reflect on the philosophical message of the text
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the core advice (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Poster: 'My Three Days'
activities_projects.objective: Apply the chapter's concept to personal life
steps: If you had to choose 3 things to see for the last time, what would they be?
Draw these 3 things on a poster
Give your poster the title: 'Appreciating the World'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students express personal values through creative art
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the chapter's theme (10 marks)
